title: pow: relay ws.rs challenge emission + AUTHENTICATE verification
description:
Wire PoW into relay/src/ws.rs: send salt+difficulty in Challenge; verify token before accepting AUTHENTICATE.

What: Two changes to the relay's auth handshake in handle().

Part 1 — Challenge emission (before sending challenge notification):
1. Read pow_difficulty = state.pow_difficulty()  (returns u8; 0 = disabled)
2. Encode server_salt to base64: B64.encode(state.pow_server_salt())
3. Build ChallengeParams { nonce: nonce.clone(), server_salt: salt_b64, difficulty: pow_difficulty }
4. Send as before

Part 2 — AUTHENTICATE handler (after extracting AuthenticateParams):
1. If state.pow_difficulty() == 0: skip PoW verification (disabled)
2. If state.pow_difficulty() > 0:
   a. Let Some(token_str) = params.pow_token else { return auth_error(POW_REQUIRED) }
   b. Decode params.pub_key from base64 to pub_key_bytes (needed for verify_token)
      Note: pub_key bytes are the raw 32-byte Ed25519 verifying key
   c. Get now_unix_secs from SystemTime::now()
   d. Call nie_core::pow::verify_token(token_str, pub_key_bytes, state.pow_server_salt(), now_unix_secs, state.pow_difficulty())
   e. Match result:
      - Ok(h16): proceed; add h16 to replay set: state.inner.pow_replay_set.insert(h16, Instant::now())
      - Err(PowError::Stale): return auth_error(POW_STALE)  
      - Err(PowError::Replayed): return auth_error(POW_REPLAYED)
      - Err(_): return auth_error(POW_INVALID)
3. But ALSO add replay check before calling verify_token:
   if state.inner.pow_replay_set.get(&h16_from_token) is Some: return auth_error(POW_REPLAYED)
   Actually verify_token does NOT have access to the replay set — the relay checks replay after verify_token returns the h16.
   
   Revised algorithm:
   a. Decode and call verify_token → get Ok(h16) or Err
   b. If Ok(h16): check replay: state.inner.pow_replay_set.contains_key(&h16)
      If in set → return auth_error(POW_REPLAYED)
      If not → insert into set and continue
4. Continue to verify_challenge (signature check) as before
5. Existing cleanup: periodically evict expired entries from pow_replay_set.
   Strategy: lazy eviction — on each PoW verification attempt, remove entries older than 600s.
   This avoids a background task and keeps the code simple.

Error helper: auth_error(code) sends JsonRpcResponse with error {code, message} and returns.
Use rpc_errors::POW_REQUIRED, POW_INVALID, POW_STALE, POW_REPLAYED from protocol.rs.

Defensive programming:
- pub_key decode happens before PoW verification; on decode error return AUTH_FAILED (same as now)
- Replay set lazy eviction: iterate set and remove entries older than 600s on every verify call
- Never log server_salt or pub_key bytes
- PoW check happens before signature check — fail fast on bad token format
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-relay passes; challenge sends server_salt+difficulty; AUTHENTICATE verifies token or rejects with appropriate error code
close_reason: ws.rs: challenge includes salt/difficulty from AppState; AUTHENTICATE handler verifies PoW token (required/invalid/stale/replayed) before signature check
